title: Dicranopteris lessonii
taxonomy: Dicranopteris lessonii is a species of fern in the family Matoniaceae. It belongs to the genus Dicranopteris, which is a group of vascular plants often referred to as 'water ferns' or 'shingiwara' in some cultures. The species Dicranopteris lessonii is native to tropical regions around the world, including Asia and the Pacific Islands. As a vascular plant, Dicranopteris lessonii is classified as a type of pteridophyte, which is a division of plants that includes ferns and their allies. Dicranopteris lessonii's classification also includes the kingdom Plantae, which is a broad category of organisms that includes all plants.
uses: Dicranopteris lessonii is also known as the "queens fern" and has been used in traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. Its leaves are traditionally used to make a poultice that is applied to the affected area to relieve pain and inflammation. The plant also has antibacterial and antifungal properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In some cultures, the plant is also used as a natural remedy for digestive issues.

side_effects: Dicranopteris lessonii is a plant species also known as the 'queens fern' or 'cabbage fern', and like many herbal remedies, it can have potential side effects.
